data.title: Assessing Aktis Oncology (AKTS) Valuation After Recent Share Price Movement And Ongoing Losses
data.description:
Advertisement

Event context and why Aktis Oncology is on investors’ radar

Aktis Oncology (AKTS) recently drew attention after a daily share move of about 3%. This has prompted investors to look more closely at how its clinical stage radiopharmaceutical pipeline and current financial profile line up.

See our latest analysis for Aktis Oncology.

While the latest 1 day share price return of 2.90% was negative, the year to date share price return of 20.83% suggests earlier momentum that investors are now reassessing as clinical progress and funding needs come into focus.

If Aktis Oncology has caught your eye, it can help to see what else is moving in the sector, including other healthcare stocks that may fit a similar high research and development profile.

With Aktis Oncology posting a 20.83% year-to-date gain but still reporting just US$5.56 million in revenue against a US$60.649 million loss, is the current price a potential entry point, or is the market already banking on future growth?

Price-to-Book of -8.6x: Is it justified?

Aktis Oncology currently reports negative equity, which results in a P/B ratio of -8.6x compared with a peer average of 3.8x and a US Biotechs industry average of 2.7x.

P/B usually compares a company’s market value with the accounting value of its net assets, and it is often used for asset heavy or early stage businesses where earnings are not yet a reliable guide. When equity turns negative, this ratio becomes harder to interpret, because the denominator is below zero and no longer reflects a typical asset base.

For Aktis Oncology, the combination of negative equity and a P/B of -8.6x suggests the balance sheet structure is very different from the average biotech peer, which may reflect accumulated losses, funding structure or both. With 100% of liabilities coming from higher risk sources of funding and the company still reporting a US$60.649 million loss, the usual P/B comparison to peers at 3.8x and the wider industry at 2.7x does not point to a straightforward discount or premium story.

Given these factors, P/B here is more a flag to review the capital structure and funding profile than a clean valuation shortcut when you line it up next to other US biotech names.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Book of -8.6x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, you still have to weigh clinical and regulatory setbacks, as well as ongoing funding needs, given US$5.56 million in revenue against a US$60.649 million loss.

Find out about the key risks to this Aktis Oncology narrative.

If your view of Aktis Oncology differs, or if you prefer to work from the raw numbers yourself, you can build a custom thesis in just a few minutes by starting with Do it your way.

data.title: Thomson Reuters (TSX:TRI) Valuation Check After Launch Of Trust In AI Alliance
data.description:
Thomson Reuters (TSX:TRI) has launched the Trust in AI Alliance through Thomson Reuters Labs, partnering with Anthropic, AWS, Google Cloud, and OpenAI to focus on trustworthy, agentic AI systems in high-stakes professional settings.

See our latest analysis for Thomson Reuters.

The Trust in AI Alliance announcement comes after a softer patch in the share price, with a 20.07% 90 day share price return decline and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 18.13%. However, the 5 year total shareholder return of 88.19% points to much stronger longer term momentum.

If AI driven tools in legal, tax, and compliance are on your radar, this could be a good moment to widen your watchlist using high growth tech and AI stocks.

So, with Thomson Reuters shares giving back some recent gains despite solid 3-year and 5-year total returns, and the market already aware of its AI push, is there still a potential entry point here, or are investors already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 32.8% Undervalued

With Thomson Reuters last closing at CA$178.86 against an implied fair value of about US$265.98 in the most followed narrative, the valuation gap depends heavily on how far its AI centric workflow tools can reshape earnings power.

The acute talent shortage in tax, accounting, and audit professions is increasing reliance on workflow automation and AI-powered tools. Thomson Reuters' advanced solutions are positioned to address this structural demand, expanding the addressable market and driving accelerated growth in its Tax & Accounting segment.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what kind of revenue runway and profit margins this narrative is incorporating, and what future earnings multiple it assumes? The underlying numbers present a very specific view of how recurring subscriptions, higher priced AI add ons, and disciplined cost of capital could justify that gap to fair value.

Result: Fair Value of $265.98 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this story can change quickly if AI adoption stays slow or if acquisitions like SafeSend and Pagero create integration issues that weigh on margins.

Find out about the key risks to this Thomson Reuters narrative.

Another Angle On Valuation

Our DCF model suggests Thomson Reuters, at CA$178.86, is trading about 28.2% below an estimated fair value of CA$249.25. This also points to undervaluation. When both the narrative based fair value and the cash flow model lean the same way, it raises a simple question: what risk are you being paid to take here?

data.title: Assessing XtalPi Holdings (SEHK:2228) Valuation After Strong AI Rally And Recent Profit Taking
data.description:
Advertisement

Why XtalPi’s recent bond deal matters for equity investors

XtalPi Holdings (SEHK:2228) recently announced a HK$2.866b zero coupon bond offering, while its shares saw profit taking after a strong past 3 months, as investors reassess AI drug discovery exposure.

The bond is an unsubordinated, unsecured, callable zero coupon note priced at 100% of principal and due January 26, 2027. It was issued under Regulation S, which keeps equity investors focused on how the company allocates this fresh capital.

See our latest analysis for XtalPi Holdings.

That bond issue lands at a time when sentiment around AI drug discovery is shifting quickly. XtalPi’s 7 day share price return of 20.74% and 30 day share price return of 41.58% point to strong near term momentum, while its 1 year total shareholder return of 196.91% shows how much the story has already moved.

If this kind of AI driven move has your attention, it could be a moment to scan high growth tech and AI stocks to spot other names riding similar themes or potentially setting up for a next leg.

With the share price up 196.91% over the past year and trading above the HK$10.26 analyst target at HK$13.45, you have to ask: is XtalPi still mispriced, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Price-to-book of 7.4x, is it justified?

At HK$13.45, XtalPi is carrying a P/B of 7.4x, which puts it well above both its Asian life sciences peers and its direct comparables.

P/B compares the market value of the equity to the accounting book value of net assets. This ratio is particularly relevant for a business that is still loss making and investing heavily in research and platforms rather than hard assets.

With XtalPi currently unprofitable, a 7.4x P/B indicates that investors are assigning a relatively high value to future earnings potential and to asset-light AI and robotics platforms that may not be fully reflected on the balance sheet.

Against an Asian life sciences industry average of 2.5x and a peer average of 4.1x, the current P/B represents materially richer pricing and suggests the market is already paying a premium for growth expectations.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-book of 7.4x (OVERVALUED).

However, you also need to weigh risks such as XtalPi’s HK$196.795m net loss and the possibility that enthusiasm for AI drug discovery cools faster than expected.

Find out about the key risks to this XtalPi Holdings narrative.

Another view on value

While the 7.4x P/B suggests a rich price tag, our DCF model comes out even tougher. At HK$13.45, XtalPi trades very far above the HK$4.83 DCF fair value estimate, which frames today’s price as overvalued. This raises the question of whether the share price is leaning more on story than on cash flows.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 883 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. data.title: Did Barclays’ Upgrade and EQT’s Exit Just Shift Kodiak Gas Services' (KGS) Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• In recent days, Barclays upgraded Kodiak Gas Services to an Overweight rating, while the company announced a dual listing on NYSE Texas and the full exit of EQT Infrastructure’s remaining stake via an underwritten public offering.
  • These moves not only underscore sustained demand for Kodiak’s compression services tied to natural gas volume growth, but also mark a meaningful shift in its shareholder base and governance influence.
  • We’ll now examine how Barclays’ more positive stance, alongside EQT Infrastructure’s exit, may influence Kodiak Gas Services’ existing investment narrative.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

Kodiak Gas Services Investment Narrative Recap

To own Kodiak Gas Services, you need to be comfortable with a capital intensive compression business that depends on healthy natural gas volumes and solid fleet utilization. Barclays’ upgrade and the dual listing do not materially change the near term demand catalyst, but EQT Infrastructure’s full exit sharpens attention on execution risks such as labor availability in the Permian and the company’s ability to sustain margins in a concentrated, large horsepower segment.

The most relevant recent move here is EQT Infrastructure’s underwritten public offering of its remaining stake, which also ends its board nomination rights. This shift in Kodiak’s shareholder base and governance framework comes as investors weigh ongoing compression demand against risks like potential overcapacity and pricing pressure if natural gas growth slows or the segment becomes more commoditized.

However, investors should also be aware that Kodiak’s focus on large horsepower compression could leave it more exposed if...

data.title: DOF Group (OB:DOFG) Deepens Petrobras Ties With AUV‑Enabled Skandi Commander Contract – What Changes?
data.description:
• DOF Group ASA recently announced a 4-year contract, worth about US$150 million, for the RSV Skandi Commander with Petrobras in Brazil, with operations set to start in January 2027.
  • This agreement is distinct because it combines an Autonomous Underwater Vehicle with a traditional Remote Operated Vehicle, highlighting DOF Group’s focus on higher‑spec, technology‑enabled offshore services.
  • We’ll now examine how this new long-term Petrobras contract for Skandi Commander, with its AUV capability, reshapes DOF Group’s investment narrative.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

DOF Group Investment Narrative Recap

To own DOF Group, you need to believe the company can convert its large subsea vessel backlog into durable cash flows while gradually reducing balance sheet risk. The new 4 year, roughly US$150 million Skandi Commander contract fits this story by extending revenue visibility in Brazil, but it does not fundamentally change the key near term swing factors: execution on existing contracts and exposure to highly concentrated Brazilian demand.

The Skandi Commander award sits alongside DOF’s earlier 4 year Petrobras contracts for Skandi Chieftain and Skandi Olympia, which together added about US$200 million of backlog. Taken together, these Brazil focused wins underline how much of DOF’s growth is currently tied to one major client and geography, reinforcing both the appeal of high utilization and premium subsea work and the importance of monitoring concentration risk.

data.title: A Look At Veracyte (VCYT) Valuation After Strong 2025 Update And 2026 Growth Guidance
data.description:
Veracyte (VCYT) shares are in focus after the company released preliminary 2025 results alongside fresh guidance for 2026, outlining expected revenue ranges, testing growth and margin targets that give investors more visibility.

See our latest analysis for Veracyte.

Veracyte shares have been volatile around the recent guidance, with a 3.35% 1 day share price return to US$43.45 and a 24.93% 90 day share price return. The 1 year total shareholder return of 5.82% contrasts with a 62.80% total shareholder return over three years, suggesting momentum has cooled but interest around the new outlook is still influencing sentiment.

If Veracyte’s update has you thinking about other opportunities in diagnostics, it could be a good moment to scan healthcare stocks that might fit your portfolio goals.

With Veracyte trading at US$43.45, at a discount to both some analyst targets and certain intrinsic value estimates, the key question is whether this is a genuine opportunity or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 8.5% Undervalued

Veracyte’s most followed narrative puts fair value at US$47.50 versus the recent US$43.45 close, so the story hinges on whether future execution matches those expectations.

Investments in automation, next-generation sequencing platforms (e.g., NovaSeq X), and data analytics, combined with strategic restructuring (SAS sale), are driving operational efficiencies and lower cost of goods, which is translating into improving gross margins and higher long-term profitability. Increasing clinical evidence, integration of AI/digital pathology, partnerships (Helix, biopharma), and a growing GRID/data platform are enhancing Veracyte's technology differentiation and providing leverage to value-based healthcare models. This strengthens the reimbursement outlook and competitive edge, supporting higher sustainable margins and earnings growth as volumes scale.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious how that efficiency story turns into a higher price tag? The narrative leans on compounded revenue growth, richer margins and a punchy future earnings multiple. Want to see how those pieces fit together and what kind of earnings profile they imply by the late 2020s?

Result: Fair Value of $47.50 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that story could fray if payer coverage for key tests like Decipher and Afirma disappoints, or if larger rivals gain ground in AI driven cancer diagnostics.

Another View: Earnings Multiple Flags a Rich Price Tag

So far the story has leaned on future cash flows and a fair value around US$47.50, yet Veracyte’s current P/E of 113.3x tells a very different story. That compares with 21.1x for the US Biotechs industry, 64.6x for peers, and a fair ratio of 24x that our work suggests the market could move toward over time.

In plain terms, the share price currently bakes in a lot of future success, which raises the risk that any stumble in growth, margins or sentiment could hit the multiple harder than the business itself. If cash flow models point to upside while the P/E looks stretched, which signal do you trust more?

data.title: A Look At Brown & Brown (BRO) Valuation As Analyst Downgrades Highlight Softer Insurance Market
data.description:
Recent analyst actions around Brown & Brown (BRO) have turned attention to how a softening property and casualty insurance market could influence the company’s organic growth expectations, margins, and investor sentiment over the next few years.

See our latest analysis for Brown & Brown.

Brown & Brown’s recent analyst downgrades and concerns about softer property and casualty pricing sit against a share price of US$79.61, with a 90 day share price return of a 16% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 21.87% loss. The 5 year total shareholder return of 77.83% suggests longer term holders have still seen substantial gains, so momentum appears weaker in the near term compared with the longer history.

If you are weighing up what to do next after this softer patch, it could be a good time to see how other insurers are positioned through healthcare stocks.

With the share price under pressure, revenue and net income growth still positive, and analysts trimming expectations, the key question is simple: is Brown & Brown now undervalued, or is the market already factoring in its future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 13.7% Undervalued

Compared with Brown & Brown’s last close of US$79.61, the most followed narrative points to a fair value in the low US$90s. This frames current weakness as a potential valuation gap rather than a permanent reset.

Strong cash flow from operations, combined with a strategic focus on leveraging a diversified portfolio across geographies and lines of business, positions the company to navigate economic uncertainties and potentially increase future earnings stability and growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what has to happen for that higher value to stack up? This narrative leans heavily on faster top line growth, resilient margins and a rich future earnings multiple. Want to see how those ingredients fit together and what discount rate is used to justify them? Read on to see the full playbook behind this fair value.

The most popular narrative applies a discount rate of 6.96% and projects Brown & Brown’s future cash generation out over several years, then brings those cash flows back to today’s terms to arrive at a fair value near US$92 per share.

Those assumptions sit alongside expectations for healthy revenue growth, slightly lower profit margins over time and a future P/E that remains above the broader US insurance peer group. Together, these factors help explain why the narrative arrives at a fair value above the current share price.

Result: Fair Value of $92.29 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this depends on economic conditions and regulation cooperating. Slower revenue growth or unfavorable legislative changes could both quickly challenge the optimistic fair value case.

The 13.7% DCF style undervaluation appears inconsistent with the earnings picture. Brown & Brown trades on a P/E of 27.5x, roughly double the US Insurance industry at 12.9x and well above its own fair ratio of 16.4x, which indicates potential valuation risk if sentiment cools.

data.title: United Spirits Limited's (NSE:UNITDSPR) Stock Has Been Sliding But Fundamentals Look Strong: Is The Market Wrong?
data.description:
It is hard to get excited after looking at United Spirits' (NSE:UNITDSPR) recent performance, when its stock has declined 8.8% over the past month. However, stock prices are usually driven by a company’s financial performance over the long term, which in this case looks quite promising. In this article, we decided to focus on United Spirits' ROE.

Return on equity or ROE is an important factor to be considered by a shareholder because it tells them how effectively their capital is being reinvested. Put another way, it reveals the company's success at turning shareholder investments into profits.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

How Is ROE Calculated?

The formula for ROE is:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for United Spirits is:

19% = ₹16b ÷ ₹84b (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the profit over the last twelve months. So, this means that for every ₹1 of its shareholder's investments, the company generates a profit of ₹0.19.

See our latest analysis for United Spirits

What Has ROE Got To Do With Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Depending on how much of these profits the company reinvests or "retains", and how effectively it does so, we are then able to assess a company’s earnings growth potential. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

United Spirits' Earnings Growth And 19% ROE

To begin with, United Spirits seems to have a respectable ROE. Further, the company's ROE compares quite favorably to the industry average of 12%. This certainly adds some context to United Spirits' exceptional 24% net income growth seen over the past five years. We reckon that there could also be other factors at play here. For instance, the company has a low payout ratio or is being managed efficiently.

We then compared United Spirits' net income growth with the industry and we're pleased to see that the company's growth figure is higher when compared with the industry which has a growth rate of 19% in the same 5-year period.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. By doing so, they will have an idea if the stock is headed into clear blue waters or if swampy waters await. Is United Spirits fairly valued compared to other companies? These 3 valuation measures might help you decide.

Is United Spirits Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

United Spirits has a three-year median payout ratio of 45% (where it is retaining 55% of its income) which is not too low or not too high. So it seems that United Spirits is reinvesting efficiently in a way that it sees impressive growth in its earnings (discussed above) and pays a dividend that's well covered.

While United Spirits has seen growth in its earnings, it only recently started to pay a dividend. It is most likely that the company decided to impress new and existing shareholders with a dividend. Our latest analyst data shows that the future payout ratio of the company over the next three years is expected to be approximately 42%. Accordingly, forecasts suggest that United Spirits' future ROE will be 22% which is again, similar to the current ROE.

Conclusion

Overall, we are quite pleased with United Spirits' performance. In particular, it's great to see that the company is investing heavily into its business and along with a high rate of return, that has resulted in a sizeable growth in its earnings. Having said that, the company's earnings growth is expected to slow down, as forecasted in the current analyst estimates. To know more about the latest analysts predictions for the company, check out this visualization of analyst forecasts for the company.

data.title: Dredging Corporation of India (NSE:DREDGECORP) shareholder returns have been impressive, earning 261% in 5 years
data.description:
When you buy a stock there is always a possibility that it could drop 100%. But on the bright side, if you buy shares in a high quality company at the right price, you can gain well over 100%. For instance, the price of Dredging Corporation of India Limited (NSE:DREDGECORP) stock is up an impressive 261% over the last five years. Also pleasing for shareholders was the 83% gain in the last three months.

Since the stock has added ₹4.8b to its market cap in the past week alone, let's see if underlying performance has been driving long-term returns.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Given that Dredging Corporation of India didn't make a profit in the last twelve months, we'll focus on revenue growth to form a quick view of its business development. Shareholders of unprofitable companies usually desire strong revenue growth. As you can imagine, fast revenue growth, when maintained, often leads to fast profit growth.

In the last 5 years Dredging Corporation of India saw its revenue grow at 9.3% per year. That's a pretty good long term growth rate. Broadly speaking, this solid progress may well be reflected by the healthy share price gain of 29% per year over five years. It's well worth monitoring the growth trend in revenue, because if growth accelerates, that might signal an opportunity. When a growth trend accelerates, be it in revenue or earnings, it can indicate an inflection point for the business, which is can often be an opportunity for investors.

The company's revenue and earnings (over time) are depicted in the image below (click to see the exact numbers).

Balance sheet strength is crucial. It might be well worthwhile taking a look at our free report on how its financial position has changed over time.

Advertisement

A Different Perspective

It's nice to see that Dredging Corporation of India shareholders have received a total shareholder return of 45% over the last year. That's better than the annualised return of 29% over half a decade, implying that the company is doing better recently. Given the share price momentum remains strong, it might be worth taking a closer look at the stock, lest you miss an opportunity. While it is well worth considering the different impacts that market conditions can have on the share price, there are other factors that are even more important. Consider for instance, the ever-present spectre of investment risk. We've identified 2 warning signs with Dredging Corporation of India , and understanding them should be part of your investment process.

data.title: Despite shrinking by ₹1.9b in the past week, Genesys International (NSE:GENESYS) shareholders are still up 438% over 5 years
data.description:
Some Genesys International Corporation Limited (NSE:GENESYS) shareholders are probably rather concerned to see the share price fall 33% over the last three months. But that doesn't change the fact that the returns over the last half decade have been spectacular. In that time, the share price has soared some 438% higher! So we don't think the recent decline in the share price means its story is a sad one. Of course what matters most is whether the business can improve itself sustainably, thus justifying a higher price. While the long term returns are impressive, we do have some sympathy for those who bought more recently, given the 61% drop, in the last year.

In light of the stock dropping 11% in the past week, we want to investigate the longer term story, and see if fundamentals have been the driver of the company's positive five-year return.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

To paraphrase Benjamin Graham: Over the short term the market is a voting machine, but over the long term it's a weighing machine. One imperfect but simple way to consider how the market perception of a company has shifted is to compare the change in the earnings per share (EPS) with the share price movement.

During the last half decade, Genesys International became profitable. That kind of transition can be an inflection point that justifies a strong share price gain, just as we have seen here. Given that the company made a profit three years ago, but not five years ago, it is worth looking at the share price returns over the last three years, too. In fact, the Genesys International stock price is 12% lower in the last three years. During the same period, EPS grew by 24% each year. So there seems to be a mismatch between the positive EPS growth and the change in the share price, which is down -4% per year.

The image below shows how EPS has tracked over time (if you click on the image you can see greater detail).

We're pleased to report that the CEO is remunerated more modestly than most CEOs at similarly capitalized companies. It's always worth keeping an eye on CEO pay, but a more important question is whether the company will grow earnings throughout the years. This free interactive report on Genesys International's earnings, revenue and cash flow is a great place to start, if you want to investigate the stock further.

Advertisement

A Different Perspective

Investors in Genesys International had a tough year, with a total loss of 61%, against a market gain of about 9.4%. Even the share prices of good stocks drop sometimes, but we want to see improvements in the fundamental metrics of a business, before getting too interested. On the bright side, long term shareholders have made money, with a gain of 40% per year over half a decade. If the fundamental data continues to indicate long term sustainable growth, the current sell-off could be an opportunity worth considering. I find it very interesting to look at share price over the long term as a proxy for business performance. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. For example, we've discovered 2 warning signs for Genesys International (1 is a bit unpleasant!) that you should be aware of before investing here.

We will like Genesys International better if we see some big insider buys. While we wait, check out this free list of undervalued stocks (mostly small caps) with considerable, recent, insider buying.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Indian exchanges.

data.title: Estimating The Fair Value Of Vimta Labs Limited (NSE:VIMTALABS)
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Vimta Labs' estimated fair value is ₹510 based on 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity
  • With ₹550 share price, Vimta Labs appears to be trading close to its estimated fair value
  • Vimta Labs' peers seem to be trading at a higher premium to fair value based onthe industry average of -393%

Today we will run through one way of estimating the intrinsic value of Vimta Labs Limited (NSE:VIMTALABS) by taking the expected future cash flows and discounting them to today's value. The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model is the tool we will apply to do this. Believe it or not, it's not too difficult to follow, as you'll see from our example!

Remember though, that there are many ways to estimate a company's value, and a DCF is just one method. If you still have some burning questions about this type of valuation, take a look at the Simply Wall St analysis model.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Is Vimta Labs Fairly Valued?

We are going to use a two-stage DCF model, which, as the name states, takes into account two stages of growth. The first stage is generally a higher growth period which levels off heading towards the terminal value, captured in the second 'steady growth' period. In the first stage we need to estimate the cash flows to the business over the next ten years. Seeing as no analyst estimates of free cash flow are available to us, we have extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the company's last reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

Generally we assume that a dollar today is more valuable than a dollar in the future, and so the sum of these future cash flows is then discounted to today's value:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026202720282029203020312032203320342035
Levered FCF (₹, Millions) ₹562.9m₹862.8m₹1.20b₹1.56b₹1.91b₹2.25b₹2.58b₹2.90b₹3.21b₹3.51b
Growth Rate Estimate SourceEst @ 73.19%Est @ 53.27%Est @ 39.33%Est @ 29.56%Est @ 22.73%Est @ 17.95%Est @ 14.60%Est @ 12.26%Est @ 10.62%Est @ 9.47%
Present Value (₹, Millions) Discounted @ 14% ₹494₹663₹810₹921₹991₹1.0k₹1.0k₹1.0k₹983₹944

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = ₹8.9b

After calculating the present value of future cash flows in the initial 10-year period, we need to calculate the Terminal Value, which accounts for all future cash flows beyond the first stage. The Gordon Growth formula is used to calculate Terminal Value at a future annual growth rate equal to the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield of 6.8%. We discount the terminal cash flows to today's value at a cost of equity of 14%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = ₹3.5b× (1 + 6.8%) ÷ (14%– 6.8%) = ₹52b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= ₹52b÷ ( 1 + 14%)10= ₹14b

The total value is the sum of cash flows for the next ten years plus the discounted terminal value, which results in the Total Equity Value, which in this case is ₹23b. The last step is to then divide the equity value by the number of shares outstanding. Compared to the current share price of ₹550, the company appears around fair value at the time of writing. Remember though, that this is just an approximate valuation, and like any complex formula - garbage in, garbage out.

The Assumptions

We would point out that the most important inputs to a discounted cash flow are the discount rate and of course the actual cash flows. Part of investing is coming up with your own evaluation of a company's future performance, so try the calculation yourself and check your own assumptions. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at Vimta Labs as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 14%, which is based on a levered beta of 0.972. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

See our latest analysis for Vimta Labs

Next Steps:

Valuation is only one side of the coin in terms of building your investment thesis, and it shouldn't be the only metric you look at when researching a company. DCF models are not the be-all and end-all of investment valuation. Instead the best use for a DCF model is to test certain assumptions and theories to see if they would lead to the company being undervalued or overvalued. For example, changes in the company's cost of equity or the risk free rate can significantly impact the valuation. For Vimta Labs, we've compiled three further elements you should further research:

  1. Financial Health: Does VIMTALABS have a healthy balance sheet? Take a look at our free balance sheet analysis with six simple checks on key factors like leverage and risk.
  2. Other Solid Businesses: Low debt, high returns on equity and good past performance are fundamental to a strong business. Why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals to see if there are other companies you may not have considered!
  3. Other Environmentally-Friendly Companies: Concerned about the environment and think consumers will buy eco-friendly products more and more? Browse through our interactive list of companies that are thinking about a greener future to discover some stocks you may not have thought of!

PS. The Simply Wall St app conducts a discounted cash flow valuation for every stock on the NSEI every day. If you want to find the calculation for other stocks just search here.

data.title: Is Ulta Beauty (ULTA) Pricing In Too Much Good News After Strong Share Price Run
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Ulta Beauty's share price still offers value after a strong run, this article will walk through what the current market price might imply about the stock.
  • Ulta Beauty recently closed at US$661.49, with returns of 1.8% over 7 days, 10.0% over 30 days, 6.7% year to date, 63.7% over 1 year, 33.7% over 3 years, and 125.6% over 5 years. These figures naturally raise questions about what is already priced in and how much risk you are taking on at these levels.
  • Recent coverage has continued to focus on Ulta Beauty's position in the US beauty retail market and how investors are thinking about its long term growth potential and competitive strengths. That backdrop is important context when assessing whether the recent share price performance lines up with what the fundamentals might support.
  • Simply Wall St currently gives Ulta Beauty a value score of 1 out of 6. We will break this down using several valuation approaches before coming back to a broader, and arguably more useful, way to think about what the stock is really worth.

Ulta Beauty scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Ulta Beauty Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth today by projecting future cash flows and then discounting those back to a single present value figure.

For Ulta Beauty, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $952.2 million, and analysts provide explicit projections out to 2027, with an estimated free cash flow of $1,011.2 million in that year. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates out to 2035, with annual free cash flows in the model remaining below $1.3b and discounted back to today.

Pulling those projections together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $379.67 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $661.49, the model implies the stock is around 74.2% above this estimate, which points to a rich valuation on these cash flow assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Ulta Beauty may be overvalued by 74.2%. Discover 883 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Ulta Beauty.

Approach 2: Ulta Beauty Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Ulta Beauty, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. This makes it a straightforward check against the more detailed DCF work.

What counts as a "normal" P/E really depends on what the market expects for earnings growth and how risky those earnings are. Faster and more predictable growth can justify a higher P/E, while slower or less certain growth usually lines up with a lower P/E.

Ulta Beauty currently trades on a P/E of 24.66x. That sits above the Specialty Retail industry average P/E of 20.85x and below a peer group average of 37.01x, so the market is putting a higher multiple on Ulta Beauty than on the industry as a whole, but not as high as some peers.

Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio for Ulta Beauty is 17.46x. This is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might make sense given the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics. It aims to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the industry average, which do not fully capture those differences.

Comparing Ulta Beauty's actual P/E of 24.66x to the Fair Ratio of 17.46x suggests the shares are pricing in more optimism than this framework would support.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1443 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Ulta Beauty Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which are simply your story about a company, linked directly to your assumptions for future revenue, earnings, margins and a fair value that you can compare to today’s price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives let you set out why you think Ulta Beauty might justify, for example, a fair value close to US$680 if you focus on wellness expansion, digital investments, loyalty strength and new markets, or closer to US$405 if you are more focused on rising costs, competition and the Target partnership ending. The platform then turns those views into a forecast and a fair value that updates automatically when fresh news or earnings arrive, so you can quickly see whether your fair value still sits above or below the current share price and decide how you want to act on that gap.

Do you think there's more to the story for Ulta Beauty? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.description:
Advertisement

The valuation seems to price in that Borr Drilling's strong recent contract momentum, particularly in Mexico, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia, will translate into persistently high day rates and utilization. This view may underestimate the lingering risks from oversupply in the jack-up market and the increased volume of transitional or short-duration contracts, which could compress both future revenues and margins if the anticipated demand does not fully materialize.

data.title: How Investors Are Reacting To CGI (TSX:GIB.A) Expanding Secure Defense IT With LiveDrop Collaboration
data.description:
• On 12 January 2026, LiveDrop announced it is working with CGI to support Dutch Ministry of Defense projects by integrating its software-only, radio-frequency-free optical data transfer technology with CGI’s mission-critical IT and system integration capabilities.
  • This collaboration strengthens CGI’s presence in secure, multi-domain defense IT, enhancing its role in handling sensitive information when conventional networks are unreliable or unavailable.
  • Next, we will examine how this defense-focused data transfer collaboration with LiveDrop may influence CGI’s existing investment narrative and risk profile.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 30 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

CGI Investment Narrative Recap

To own CGI, you generally need to believe in its role as a long term partner for complex digital transformation and mission critical IT, underpinned by solid cash generation and disciplined capital returns. The LiveDrop collaboration fits this narrative by reinforcing CGI’s position in secure, multi domain defense IT, but it does not materially change the key near term catalyst of converting its large backlog into higher margin revenue, nor the ongoing risk from cyclical government and enterprise spending decisions.

The LiveDrop news sits alongside other recent contract wins that highlight CGI’s focus on mission critical, sticky client relationships, such as its agreement with the Texas Department of Information Resources to provide CGI Advantage ERP and related software. Together, these kinds of deals support the catalyst of growing recurring and IP based revenue, while also reminding investors that heavy exposure to government contracts ties results to shifting budget priorities and rebid outcomes.

Yet, behind this solid contract momentum, investors should be aware of the concentration risk around large public sector clients and...

Set against that diversity, the key risk many overlook is CGI’s reliance on government and large enterprise budgets, which can directly affect how consistently that sizeable backlog turns into earnings and cash flow over time.

data.title: A Look At Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) Valuation After Another Quarterly Distribution Increase
data.description:
Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) is back in focus after its general partner’s board approved a 2.8% increase in the quarterly cash distribution to $0.55 per unit, or $2.20 annualized, for the fourth quarter of 2025.

See our latest analysis for Enterprise Products Partners.

The distribution hike comes after a steady few months for the units, with the latest share price at $32.09 and a 90 day share price return of 4.22%. The 1 year total shareholder return of 5.58% and 5 year total shareholder return of 102.48% suggest that long term income and reinvested payouts have been important drivers, pointing to gradually building rather than fading momentum.

If this kind of dependable cash flow story appeals to you, it could be a good moment to widen your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

The completion of two gas processing plants in the Permian, along with several key pipeline and export terminal projects, is expected to enhance Enterprise Products Partners’ infrastructure, potentially driving revenue growth from increased volume handling and exports. With no major planned downtimes for the PDH plants after recent maintenance, Enterprise is poised to capture additional EBITDA that was previously lost to unplanned outages, suggesting potential earnings improvement.

data.title: Assessing Argan (AGX) Valuation After Insider Sale Dividend Hike And Earnings Beat
data.description:
Argan (AGX) is back on investor watchlists after Director Peter W Getsinger sold roughly US$3.38 million in shares, shortly after the company lifted its quarterly cash dividend by 33% and reported earnings ahead of expectations.

See our latest analysis for Argan.

Those events come after a sharp run, with Argan’s 1 year total shareholder return close to 100% and multi year total shareholder returns several times higher, even as recent share price returns have softened and short term momentum has cooled.

If Argan’s recent moves have caught your eye, it could be a good moment to see what else is out there and scan fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

Most Popular Narrative: 14.3% Undervalued

With Argan last closing at US$309.26 versus a narrative fair value of US$361.00, the story centers on how future projects could support that gap.

Record backlog and continued project wins across gas, renewables, water treatment, and recycling plants provide multi-year revenue visibility, indicating potential for increased operating leverage and higher gross margins as larger projects are executed successfully.
